mapping.a_element: hydrodynamic behavior of finite-temperature field theory and its low-momentum limit of correlation functions
mapping.b_element: thermodynamic interpretation of gravitational field equations and entropy maximization principle

mapping.a_element: viscosity/entropy ratio and its universality in theories with gravity duals
mapping.b_element: horizon entropy and its relation to Noether charge in gravitational dynamics

mapping.a_element: low-lying quasinormal modes of the AdS black p-brane metric
mapping.b_element: thermodynamic properties associated with horizons (e.g., temperature, entropy)

mapping.a_element: AdS/CFT correspondence as a duality between field theory and gravity
mapping.b_element: connection between horizon thermodynamics and gravitational dynamics

brief_justification: Both papers explore the deep connection between gravitational dynamics and thermodynamic principles, particularly entropy and temperature associated with horizons. Paper A uses the AdS/CFT framework to derive hydrodynamic properties (e.g., viscosity) from gravitational duals, while Paper B derives gravitational field equations from thermodynamic entropy maximization, showing that the underlying machinery in both is the equivalence of gravitational and thermodynamic descriptions.
